item.title: Ah, the good ol' 3008.
item.content:encoded: Everyone has heard about the Infinite Ikea. I came in expecting an alright article that was made popular only because of the memes, came out genuinely surprised at how well written this was. Nice stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Sorry for the necro) I agree, as I want to know more about this. Where does it all come from? Do the Staff stock the stores or does it just appear there? Why do more staff appear near staff corpses? And maybe, just maybe, is there a way one of the exits can be stabilized or information from the outside world can be sent in to assist the survivors?
Surely if they can pilot a quadcopter or any other sort of drone in there, they could get more information to form addendums and other expansions to this document.
Then again, all of these questions receiving answers also requires someone (likely the original author, Mortos) to author them and decide to conclusively how it should be.

item.title: Re: But this makes no sense
item.content:encoded: This is an SCP. Why are you questioning their ability to talk without faces? The SCP is an infinite IKEA realm for God's sake.

item.title: Love it!
item.content:encoded:
Just found this one by chance when someone referenced it on a Twitter thread titled "IKEA: the anime" and love it. I think I like it almost as much as "Granny" (SCP-517, the "Grandmother Predictions" fortune-telling machine) which, considering 517 is my second-favorite at this point (the first is SCP-1549/"belowaverage.js", a quantum-tunneling JavaScript), that's saying a lot. Seriously, an IKEA so big (and slightly supernatural) that people from multiple timelines get lost and form a civilization inside? I think what gets me is how plausible that is… like the guy didn't even realize he had been teleported until he tried to leave, because it was just so similar to a normal IKEA (Though calling any IKEA "normal" might be a bit misleading…).
Speaking of which, my take on this is that in one of those timelines, the world was just different enough that the IKEA store in that specific retail park became sentient and figured out how to connect to its parallel versions and link them all together internally (thus why it's virtually infinite). That's all it is: a bunch of normal IKEAs stitched together across the multiverse and imbued with the company culture as a consciousness. "The store is now closed, please exit the building".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No mention if the journalist and others that exited successfully were identified, but since they were released, I'd presume so. I mean - you wouldn't normally release visitors from different dimensions to wander around.

Of course, The Foundation may not have realized they were from other dimensions until well after release.

item.title: Re: Here's the ELI5
item.content:encoded: But IKEA has meatballs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fantastic SCP all through, and IDK if it was previously mentioned, but the fact that a box of supplies had a piece of the staff in there tells me that, unfortunate as it is for our protagonist mentality, someone at Ikea is looking at ways to destroy other settlements. Maybe it's just my headcanon, but I find it interesting. Who would knowingly put a piece of staff in a supply box and then trade it away?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's my favourite SCP.
Why can't give 2 upvote?

item.title: Re: The journal guy became the staff
item.content:encoded:
Relevant portion:

At 00:37 on ██/██/200█ a human male exited SCP-3008, followed 10 seconds later by an instance of SCP-3008-2. SCP-3008-2 caught and killed the man before itself being terminated by armed response personnel.

That's a cool twist but unfounded.

item.title: Re: The journal guy became the staff
item.content:encoded: That is incorrect.

item.title: The journal guy became the staff
item.content:encoded: Just so we're clear, the guy who wrote the journal is the staff they shoot and retrieve the journal from

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Look who just turned 2000! Happy birthday, crazy IKEA scip!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this as I, out of interest, looked at the top-rated all time pages and saw this sticking out from among the eries Is and I was like "huh?"

Can see why. IKEA is… Yeah. I only went to one for the first time myself a few years ago, and my impression was immediately "it's like biomes of furniture," which in my case, lead to the plot of a RPG quest, where the player characters explored a long-abandoned alien space-robot IKEA, so I can definitely get the inspiration.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made a video adaptation of this report on my Youtube channel Pr Mura.

I just wanted to share with you the pictures that I took for the video. Another format of the journal. ;)

+ Here are the pictures
- Here are the pictures

item.title: My Favorite SCP
item.content:encoded: This is such a cool SCP. My favorite thing about this SCP is how there are civilizations inside the Ikea, I just think that's a really awesome concept and makes you think how many people are actually in the Ikea. You could make a TV show just based on this SCP alone. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think a justification would be the interdimensional travel possibilities. Using 3008 to get resources, D class or even some thing that would help kill something like 682.

item.title: Re: dun nun nun nun X POST
item.content:encoded:
interesting idea, if each person could carry huge amounts of resources, you might make a caravan for a super city, infinity meatballs!

I actually wonder if you could make a person line from the entrance, and if you all hold hands will the area "stabilize" and then you can find your way back? that would be interesting.

But a city inside of an IKEA is interesting.

item.title: Re: dun nun nun nun X POST
item.content:encoded:
A bit of explanation:
In the SCP-001 Proposal of Locke, everyone outside is fucked, and going outside is the worst thing to do. So, what if we, well… Just didn't? Instead of Site-19, we run to SCP-3008, with everything we can carry. Ammunition, building supplies, concrete, radios, mini smelters, how to guides… And establish something like Omega Site X, for however many pop up. In the same vein, if the world ends, why not hide in Infinite Ikea? It's probably the only place we can hide and is near guaranteed to work.

item.title: dun nun nun nun X POST
item.content:encoded: 3008 is possibly thaumiel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that it would be really cool if you could delve more into the societys and the staff stocking the shelves and cooking the food.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Totally agree! I'm a big fan of what I call 'exploratory' SCP articles and stories, and I've been making a list of the best ones, like this one, for my own benefit but also because I've been tinkering with the idea of offering to make up a list of such articles on the 'User-Curated Lists' page, if there's enough interest.

Some random examples: SCP-093 "Red Sea Object", with the amazing journeys into the hellish world it provides access to; SCP-1689 "Bag Of Holding Potatoes", and the thrilling adventures in potato world; and SCP-2935 "O, Death".

I have about another 30 possible articles noted so far.

I know I'm necroposting here, but if anyone reading this has any suggestions or feedback, I'd love to hear it!

EDIT: Since posting this, I've found an awesome source of 'exploration' articles already compiled: choose Tales from the banner menu, then incident reports, then the explorations tab. There are currently 49 entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not leaving until I get my Swedish meatballs.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I you have ever been to an IKEA, this is way too relateable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care to read 240+ comments to see if this speculation has already taken place, but it occurs to me that, given enough time, the population of people trapped in there would eventually become the IKEA equivalent to a Kardeshev Type III civilization and this tickles me to no end

item.title: Re: Exploratory drones
item.content:encoded: Late, but methinks they're automaded and programmed to track down exits

item.title: Re: Amazing
item.content:encoded: I ctrl+f'd because I thought I missed it, but I didn't find it, hgelp?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fucking awesome.

Normally when reading long skips I'm like "yeah we get it move on"

Now I looked in SCP Series 4 - Tales Edition. Sadly there's only one.
But this lacks an image, is it that hard to get an Ikea image?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that seems like a wreckless and terrible idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. The sensation of hopelessness and otherworldliness in the journal entries is very heavy and I got very immersed in the story. To become one of my favorite SCPs for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does it feel to have a game and an animated series featuring you SECOND SCP. You, my sir, are a legend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a strange concept for a tale after reading this. It seems it might be a solution worth the foundation's consideration as to the subject of SCP 682's containment. Theoretically, if fourteen people out of thousands have escaped, and there is a seemingly infinite amount of faceless murder-staff, this could quite possibly prove to be a means of detaining (or at least occupying) the lizard until it found the entrance or exhausted the staff supply (if the staff are an exhaustible resource).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
in the journal it says that there is a possibility that everyone came from different dimensions, because of this fact I started thinking what if instead of being separated from his group they all went through the exit but were each transported to their original dimension instead of coming out together.
¯\_(ツ)_/¯ something to think about

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love every part of this, fantastic article. Playing on IKEAs going on forever, while also having Doctor Who esk monsters that hunt you at night. Right up my street.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The main idea behind it sounds like a joke, but the Journal and the "employee" humanoids definitely made this SCP very scary to think about. One of my favourite SCPs so far.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yea this was great.

So i assume someone planted that box in the town.
I mean we did get mention about people going and leaving and about some third party.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so good.

I mean, it's at a 1000+ rating, so that's a given, but still. So good.

I managed to catch this one a day or two after it was first posted, and it's stuck with me hard ever since. Every time we head into Ikea, it's at the front of my mind. The world building of the strange scavenger civilizations of the Infinikea just hooked my imagination. It makes me want to see more, even though I know it'd ruin the impact of the article.

TL;DR Easily one of my Top 5 posts on this site, and something I still think about often almost two years after first reading it.

item.title: Approved by the author
item.content:encoded: I feel so honoured :-)

item.title: Re: Idea
item.content:encoded: Hah, I like that, especially the christmas songs bit. If I ever write anything else there I might work that in!

item.title: Idea
item.content:encoded:
Headcannon: Certain parts of the store play music over the intercomes in accordance to the seasons and time of the year. Christmas songs would play in the winter and music themed around the seasons would play to reflect the passage of time.

Essentially, the music would be the Ikeas 'weather' (Welcome To Nightvale reference unintended).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP with a nice depth, +1 for hoping an exploration log by D-Class or MTF like what happened in 3311 or even a possible connection between these two.

item.title: love it
item.content:encoded: this is my fav scp keep it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kept hearing about this SCP and knew the idea of it, but finally reading it… damn, it's really good. I didn't think an infinite ikea could be creepy, and yet here we are! Great SCP, hope to see more stuff like this! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. Best SCP I have read yet.
I would like some background story for the IKEA and its creation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome! The journal was great! This would definitely make a good book

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is me defending an incredibly popular article from a comment from months ago, but I do feel this needs proper clarification: all of these are written whenever the protagonist felt like actually writing. That means he has the benefit of hindsight with him. Also, given how extremely bizarre the scenario is, one could guess either his mindset isn't all that comparable to most lost people or that he's using humour as a coping mechanism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incidentally, IKEA's Swedish meatballs are actually Turkish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally haven't visited an IKEA store myself, but I've gotten some meatballs from their cafeteria to-go from a visit a relative had. After reading this, I feel like I've developed some sort of fear of the place now… I wonder if this article is based off a personal experience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this'll shit me right up next time I have to take a near-closing-time visit to the Swedish Labyrinth.

Particularly I'll be looking upwards to see how high the ceilings are and if the walls actually reach all the way up, because I don't think I've ever actually checked. One of the things that makes getting lost in there so easy, after all, is that the internal walls are deliberately arranged into a sort-of-mazelike pattern (even the maps they give you exacerbate this because they split a single floor, whose pathway bounces off a central nexus point, across two sides of the same bit of paper) where you never have a line of sight extending for more than about 20 metres at a time. There's almost always a turn somewhere, making it impossible to get a clear sense of how far you've come, or indeed how much further you have to go, and the frequent 90 degree (or even 180 degree) turns don't make life any easier.

Of course, this is in the local stores I'm used to (which I STILL need a map to find my way around, every single goddamn time; it's like it exceeds the human brain's mapping capacity), where the main showroom parts are all on an upstairs floor, and the warehouse, checkouts, and main food shop section are downstairs (after entering, you almost immediately go up a rather tall escalator). Maybe in places where they have even more land to build on, it's all one level and so has a crazy high ceiling with limited-height partitions?

…think I'll try to figure it out from what I can see before going up the escalator, in fact…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The wonderful everyday. Noboddy said anything about every night…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so dayum fun. Thanks for making it. Creatively stimulating on so many levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there should be some sort of story for this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like Lord Bung's gonna make an entire episode of Confinement about this scip.

:D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something about the phrase 'row after endless row of high quality Swedish furniture' is just fucking hilarious.

item.title: Re: Amazing
item.content:encoded: … do we know that he didn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wish you did an exploration log.

Also, you should add them using 3008 on the 682 termination log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IKEA: the wonderful everyday. Sounds like the Staff have never heard that slogan before….

item.title: Re: But this makes no sense
item.content:encoded:
Dr B Baker

Exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was pretty sure it was because he was technically shoplifting the notebook.

item.title: Re: +1000
item.content:encoded: I introduced my mother to SCP-3008 because she was taking me to IKEA.
